performed_via_github_app.name: Netlify
performed_via_github_app.description:
Publish incredibly high performance websites and applications right from GitHub. The Netlify platform connects your repositories to an all-in-one workflow for global CDN deployment, continuous integration, and automatic (and free) HTTPS. Each time you commit changes, Netlify builds your site, prerenders markup, and optimizes assets on dedicated build infrastructure. Easily create an entirely automated workflows for your web applications

## Features

### Push your site live, directly from GitHub
Deploy your site to an ultra-redundant global CDN that’s purpose built for serving pages and assets quickly and consistently.

### Automate deployment
Netlify’s built-in Continuous Deployment automatically runs your build commands and deploys the result whenever you push to your Git repository.

### Add a custom domain
Purchase domains and manage DNS zones and records right inside of Netlify.

### HTTPS is automatic
Your site will automatically be secured with a free TLS certificate from Let’s Encrypt.

### Deploy Previews
Netlify’s Deploy Previews streamlined your workflow by giving you a unique, permanent URL to check what your changes will be like in production whenever you submit a pull request.

### And a lot more!
Netlify includes solutions for forms, identity, and even custom functions powered by AWS Lambda. And all this functionality can be configured directly in your repo.

body:
Greptile Overview

Greptile Summary

This PR introduces module augmentation support to allow users to define custom toast types via TypeScript's `declare module` pattern. The implementation extracts the `type` property from `ToastObject` into a separate type system using conditional types. **Key changes:** - Introduced `UserToastObject` empty interface for module augmentation - Split `ToastObject` into `BaseToastObject` and `ToastTypeObject` - Created `ToastType` export used across all toast components - Updated type annotations in `ToastAction`, `ToastClose`, `ToastDescription`, `ToastRoot`, and `ToastTitle` to use `ToastType` **Critical issue:** The conditional type logic on lines 103-107 of `useToastManager.ts` has a fundamental flaw. When users augment `UserToastObject` with a required `type` property (e.g., `type: 'success' | 'error'`), the condition `UserToastObject extends BaseToastType` will always fail because TypeScript's subtype rules require that a type with a required property cannot extend a type with an optional property. This means module augmentation will not work as intended, and `ToastType` will always resolve to `string | undefined` instead of the user's narrowed type. **Recommendations:** - Fix the conditional type by changing the constraint to `Partial` to allow both required and optional type properties - Add JSDoc documentation with working examples of module augmentation - Add tests to verify the module augmentation pattern works correctly

Confidence Score: 2/5

- This PR should not be merged without fixing the conditional type logic, as the module augmentation feature will not work as intended - The implementation contains a critical TypeScript logic error that prevents the module augmentation pattern from functioning. While the other file changes are straightforward type annotation updates that appear safe, the core feature being introduced is broken due to incorrect conditional type logic. - Pay close attention to `packages/react/src/toast/useToastManager.ts` lines 103-107 where the conditional type logic must be fixed

Important Files Changed

| Filename | Overview | |----------|----------| | packages/react/src/toast/useToastManager.ts | Adds module augmentation pattern for toast types, but the conditional type logic has a critical flaw that prevents it from working as intended |

performed_via_github_app.name: Greptile Apps
performed_via_github_app.description: Greptile is an AI agent that reviews pull requests with full context of your codebase. It can catch hidden bugs, security vulnerabilities, and anti-patterns.

body:
Thanks for looking into this. I agree that a need for custom types is real: `type` is a public styling/rendering field, so design systems may want literal completion and validation for it.

I don’t think module augmentation through `UserToastObject` is right, though. It makes toast types global, and it doesn’t account for existing built-in `promise()` behavior, which writes `loading`, `success`, and `error` to `type` and treats `loading` specially.

I’d prefer a typed-manager approach instead, probably a second generic on `useToastManager`, `createToastManager`, `ToastObject`, and the add/update/promise option types, backed by type tests. Let’s not merge this PR in its current form.

CC @atomiks

Thanks for the detailed review and for taking the time to look into this.

This was the wrong approach on my side. I appreciate the feedback and the direction here.

I’ll close this PR in its current form.
